heading: Risk communication
label: 360bbb–6 Risk communication
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (a)(4). substituted “” for “Section 14 of the Federal Advisory Committee Act”.Pub. L. 117–286Section 1013 of title 5
amendments: Amendments2022—Subsec. (a)(4). substituted “” for “Section 14 of the Federal Advisory Committee Act”.Pub. L. 117–286Section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: Advisory Committee on Risk Communication
children.label: a Advisory Committee on Risk Communication

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an advisory committee to be known as the “Advisory Committee on Risk Communication” (referred to in this section as the “Committee”).

children.heading: Duties of Committee
children.label: 2 Duties of Committee
children.text: The Committee shall advise the Commissioner on methods to effectively communicate risks associated with the products regulated by the Food and Drug Administration.

children.heading: Members
children.label: 3 Members
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the Committee is composed of experts on risk communication, experts on the risks described in subsection (b), and representatives of patient, consumer, and health professional organizations.

children.heading: Permanence of Committee
children.label: 4 Permanence of Committee
children.text: shall not apply to the Committee established under this subsection.Section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall partner with professional medical societies, medical schools, academic medical centers, and other stakeholders to develop robust and multi-faceted systems for communication to health care providers about emerging postmarket drug risks.

children.label: A
children.text: account for the diversity among physicians in terms of practice, willingness to adopt technology, and medical specialty; and

children.label: B
children.text: include the use of existing communication channels, including electronic communications, in place at the Food and Drug Administration.
